title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Donovan, 59, charged with multiple offenses after allegedly assaulting a customer and employee at East Boston Burger King.
The altercation included racial slurs and physical assaults, leading to visible lacerations on a customer who declined further treatment.
Donovan pleaded not guilty at arraignment, was released on personal recognizance, and ordered to stay away from Maverick Square Burger King.

title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rayquon Brown, 29, from Canton, was charged with murder and related offenses in the killing of Xavier Bautista in Cambridge.
Xavier Bautista, a Cambridge DPW employee, was shot on July 4 near Broadway and Norfolk Street and died from two gunshot wounds.
Brown allegedly acted as a lookout, and the killing reignited debate over the discontinued ShotSpotter gunshot detection system in Cambridge.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died after a skydiving accident at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange, Massachusetts, on Sunday.
The accident occurred after a gust of wind interfered with the man's parachute, causing him to fall 30 feet.
Authorities are investigating the accident, marking the second death at Jumptown Skydiving in the past year.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's defense sought unsuccessfully to include testimony on postpartum mental illness from other women at her upcoming murder trial.
Judge Sullivan plans to seat 18 jurors for the trial, beginning jury selection in Plymouth Superior Court on July 20.
Clancy's attorney intends to argue an insanity defense, claiming her actions were influenced by postpartum mental illness and medication.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye is ranked No. 8 on ESPN's list of the NFL's top-10 quarterbacks for 2026.
Maye led the league in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage last season.
The Patriots improved to a 14-3 record and reached the Super Bowl under Maye's leadership.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Australian James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship by consuming 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Webb defeated 18-time Nathan's Famous Hot Dog Eating Contest champion Joey Chestnut, who ate 7.54 pounds of wings.
Massachusetts native Geoff Esper, a teacher and record holder for Hooters wings, finished third in the competition.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots' linebackers were elite in 2025 under Mike Vrabel, contributing to strong defense stats.
Harold Landry and Robert Spillane were key players, but the team lost K'Lavon Chaisson.
Rookie Elijah Ponder shows promise; Gabe Jacas remains unsigned but has potential to contribute.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died after an apparent drowning in Great Pond, Randolph on Sunday, according to the Norfolk District Attorney's Office.
Randolph police and fire departments responded to a 911 call about a swimmer in distress at Great Pond at approximately 4 p.m.
Firefighters located the man, transported him to a hospital where he was pronounced dead; State Police are investigating.

title: Historic Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings hit the market
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings are listed for sale at $12 million each in Greater Boston.
The properties, owned by the Fraiman family, include a third building for $16 million in Somerville.
CSB Theatres operates the theaters with long-term leases, providing stability despite the buildings being for sale.

title: Matt Turner’s loan with the Revolution extended; New England has option to buy GK from Lyon
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Matt Turner's loan with New England Revolution extended through Dec. 31, 2026, with option for 2027.
Revolution holds option to buy Turner’s contract from French club Olympique Lyonnais after second loan year.
Turner's loan extension allows New England potential to sign another Designated Player due to TAM eligibility.
